html: Okay, but that still has a limit, right? Do training costs have a limit? Everyone is in the frothy stage of this technology wave and they continue to buy more, but training the next model requires exponential increases in model sizes to get the same sorts of model performance increases, which suggests exponential cost increases, too (even ignoring temporary cost factors such as RAM price increases). You say your company will double or triple what they are paying today; how far are they willing to go? At some point they are going to have to cut developers to fund it (e.g., cut half the developers and give the survivors each an AI assistant with $180k in token budget, captured from the salary savings), but that also presupposes the productivity gains are there to support it.
